authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-61 Revision 2, Computer Security Incident Handling Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. federal incident-handling reference for coordination phases, roles, and escalation handoff expectations used by many regulated digital-service teams.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-34 Rev. 1, Contingency Planning Guide for Federal Information Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines contingency preparedness structure and recovery sequencing standards that directly constrain incident handoff closure and dependency sequencing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Dependency mapping, sequencing, and closure conditions

authoritative_sources.title: Uptime Institute Tier Standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry standard for data-center availability design and operational resilience that changes coordination urgency and closure posture by tier and redundancy expectation.

authoritative_sources.title: ASHRAE TC9.9 Data Center Environmental Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative environmental and thermal operating guidance for data centers, materially affecting facility risk scoring and coordination windows.

authoritative_sources.title: NAICS 518: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Industry terminology and record normalization source
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary classification reference to ensure terminology and artifacts remain materially within data center and cloud service operations, avoiding generic handoff behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-145, The NIST Definition of Cloud Computing
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Clarifies cloud deployment, tenant boundary, and service model terminology needed for accurate routing and ownership boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Well-Architected Framework: Incident and response guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Vendor-neutral control patterns for incident command, communication cadence, and escalation behavior in cloud operations handoffs.

authoritative_sources.title: Microsoft Azure Well-Architected Design Guide: Incident Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides official cloud operations patterns for role-based incident routing and operational continuity practices relevant to specialist handoffs.

authoritative_sources.title: Google SRE Workbook: Incident Response
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines role-driven bridge discipline, timeline records, and escalation etiquette used to validate coordination handoff quality.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 20000-1:2018 Service Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Service-management standard for change, incident, and service continuity practices that informs evidence and handoff quality expectations in operations environments.

workflow_stages.name: Coordination intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize request scope, confirm incident ownership, and verify tenant-required records before any routing or closure output is produced.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Industry terminology and record normalization source

workflow_stages.name: Dependency mapping and system-of-record routing
workflow_stages.description: Build dependency chains across facility telemetry, cloud tenancy ownership, change-management, and maintenance systems to identify required owner handoffs.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Industry terminology and record normalization source

workflow_stages.name: Conflict and ambiguity management
workflow_stages.description: Identify duplicate ownership, stale evidence, missing prerequisites, and policy conflict signals, then escalate safely using explicit blocker records.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Handoff execution and closure confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Emit handoff summary and closure confirmation only when destination owner and evidence dependencies are complete and accepted.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Tenant adaptation and precedence convergence
workflow_stages.description: Apply tenant overrides to public baseline; when public and tenant authority materially conflict, escalate and return without confident completion.
source_classes: Industry terminology and record normalization source
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Cloud tenancy architecture and ownership map
Handoff package, dependency tracker, and closure artifacts
systems_of_record: Tenant authority directory and comms ownership roster

allowed: Coordinate incident handoffs across facility and cloud teams with explicit evidence and owner checks.
Track blockers, missing prerequisites, and ownership ambiguity before routing.
Produce handoff summaries, dependency trackers, and closure readiness confirmations when evidence is complete.
escalate: Missing tenant authority or record links that affect routing outcome.
Legal, safety-critical, financial, or policy-override requests outside coordination scope.
Conflicting source and tenant policy affecting closure or escalation path.
refuse: Execution-only requests such as runbook execution, failover actions, or remediation commands.
Direct legal, clinical, financial-approval, or engineering-design decisions.
Closure confirmation when destination artifacts or owner acceptance are incomplete.

domain_failure_modes: Treating a multi-team incident as a generic ticket transfer without validating facility telemetry and cloud tenancy ownership.
Routing closure without evidence of destination acceptance, resulting in untraceable ownership state.
Overlooking duplicate ownership on critical infrastructure steps (power, cooling, and network restoration).
Conflating coordination authority with direct remediation or approval authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

required_workflow_questions.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer_in_pack: Incident platform, cloud control plane logs, facility telemetry, CMDB/configuration, authority directory, and change-management records are explicit systems of record and are required before routing or closure outputs.

required_workflow_questions.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
required_workflow_questions.answer_in_pack: Facility operations constraints (power/cooling), tenant tenancy boundaries, cross-environment handoff dependencies, and infrastructure continuity policies create additional blockers and routing semantics not present in generic coordination.

required_workflow_questions.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer_in_pack: Execution authority, financial/legal/safety approvals, and technical remediation remain in adjacent specialist lanes; this lane is constrained to coordination, evidence integrity, routing, and escalation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant cooling and power telemetry freshness tolerance
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different tenants use different maximum staleness windows before telemetry is acceptable for routing confidence.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant policy value for telemetry freshness and escalation if absent.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tenant teams may require ticket, e-signature, or CAB approval as minimum acceptance.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Package enforces tenant-supplied acceptance schema through required artifact policy.

notes: Research sources cover workflow, evidence, terminology, and boundary handling in a single pack.
Source rationale is explicit per source class and mapped directly to workflow stages.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; each ambiguity is converted into explicit tenant-required context with escalation rules.
